samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="RECEIVE">Receiving state.</state>
        <state id="OUTPUT">Output state.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="cnt" width_description="[3:0]">Counter to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received.</reg>
        <reg id="valid" width_description="[0:0]">Valid signal for the output data.</reg>
        <reg id="data" width_description="[7:0]">Data register to store the parallel output data.</reg>
        
        // Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 4'd0;
                valid <= 1'b0;
                data <= 8'd0;
            end else begin
                // Check if the input data is valid
                if (din_valid) begin
                    // Shift the data register left by one bit and insert the new serial input data bit
                    data <= {data[6:0], din_serial};
                    
                    // Increment the counter
                    cnt <= cnt + 1;
                    
                    // Check if the counter has reached 7 (indicating that 8 bits of data have been received)
                    if (cnt == 4'd7) begin
                        // Set the valid signal to indicate that the output data is valid
                        valid <= 1'b1;
                        
                        // Reset the counter to 0 after processing 8 bits of data
                        cnt <= 4'd0;
                    end
                end else begin
                    // If the input data is not valid, set the valid signal to 0
                    valid <= 1'b0;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign the parallel output data to the dout_parallel port
        assign dout_parallel = data;
        
        // Assign the valid signal to the dout_valid port
        assign dout_valid = valid;
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. Checks if the input data is valid. If it is, shifts the data register left by one bit and inserts the new serial input data bit. Increments the counter. If the counter reaches 7 (indicating that 8 bits of data have been received), sets the valid signal to indicate that the output data is valid and resets the counter to 0. If the input data is not valid, sets the valid signal to 0. Assigns the parallel output data to the dout_parallel port and assigns the valid signal to the dout_valid port.</implementation>
endmodule
